---
title: 'Class Discussion Post: Analyze the Christian Ethics of a current Wall Street Journal Article, 03/30/2022'
date: '2022-03-31T06:00:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'NoHiding.Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6646416028557998562'
blogger_filename: '/2022/03/class-discussion-post-analyze-christian.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/NoHiding.Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Class Discussion Posts'
---

<p><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fb-img-1648412335757-daa991d81ed2.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" data-original-height="720" data-original-width="720" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fb-img-1648412335757-b7478e2e6471.jpg" width="320" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><i>Facebook Meme: Attribution Unknown?</i></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><b>Quotes:</b></p><p></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="text-indent: -0.25in;">My WSJ Article, â€œDoes the
â€˜Donâ€™t Say Gayâ€™ Bill Say That?â€, is from the Opinion section, written by The
Editorial Board.</span><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftn1" name="_ftnref1" style="text-indent: -0.25in;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span></span></a>&nbsp;</li><li><span style="text-indent: -0.25in;">A quote from the text
begins my thoughts: â€œIn fact, the First Amendmentâ€¦ to keep the state out of the
business of imposing beliefs on its citizensâ€¦to protect the church from the
state, not to protect the state from the church.â€</span><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftn2" name="_ftnref2" style="text-indent: -0.25in;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span></span></a></li></ul><p></p><p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>Thoughts:</b><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>The ethics of a multicultural society. </b>The article breaks
down a law in Florida that attempts to legislate what teachers can speak about
in the classroom and what parents have access to. While the law is nuanced, and
I can see various angles from both sides, I want to address an underlying
faulty assumption driving at laws like this, that is common in my former church
traditions.&nbsp;<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>US Constitution Error: </b>The American Right and Left both get the
1<sup>st</sup> Amendment wrong, as Rae does in his quote above.<a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftn3" name="_ftnref3" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> The foundersâ€™ primary concern
was that government not be in control of legislating peopleâ€™s beliefs, morals,
or orthopraxy. By implication, per the founding fatherâ€™s, the use of government
force to impose oneâ€™s religiously held beliefs onto another is a failure to use
government well. See also the history of the Baptists, related to these rules.<a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftn4" name="_ftnref4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>Hermeneutics error:</b> the American church believes (in error)
that she is the â€œNew Israelâ€, thus views American politics through that lens
attempting to force the nationâ€™s inhabitants to come under Yahwehâ€™s sovereignty
through political force, as though this were ancient Israel, and Washington DC was
Jerusalem. Neither the Church or the USA is â€œIsraelâ€. Israel is Israel and
remains Israel. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>The USA, Babylon, and Political Advocacy:</b> A more accurate
biblical lens would be to see modern church members as Daniel and friends
living in Babylon. As exiles in Babylon, we are given opportunities to speak
truth to power, and opportunities to suffer persecution. In all cases, we are
exiles in a foreign land. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal">While we can and should speak correction to both Right and
Left powers-that-be, it should be in advocacy for basic human dignity, not to
legislate morality or belief onto people who are not already submitted to
Yahweh. We should be serving our communities, not trying to boss them around in
the name of the Bible or God.<o:p></o:p></p>

<blockquote><p class="MsoNormal">To quote NT Wright: â€œ<i>The whole point of the Kingdom of God
is Jesus has come to bear witness to the true truth, which is nonviolent. When
God wants to take charge of the world, He doesn't send in the tanks. He sends
in the poor and the meek</i>.â€<a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftn5" name="_ftnref5" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[5]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p></blockquote>

<p class="MsoNormal"><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><b>Supplemental / Constitutional Breakdown:<o:p></o:p></b></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst">1.   The US  Constitution:&nbsp;The defacto
understanding of most church folk is reflected in the quote from the text
above. Itâ€™s simply not true, or, doesnâ€™t paint the accurate nuance.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->a.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>The founding fathers of the
USA were coming out of a state-run church/monarchy, in which the King was the ruler
of government and church (still is today in the UK, though less so). <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->b.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Government Run Church
legislated the society. In other words, if a person broke a religious law, the
civil authorities could arrest them. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->c.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Baptists broke away from
early colonies because of this practice, stating that the government had no
right to enforce its ideas of morality and spirituality on its people. This
lead, in part, to the founding of Rhode Island. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->d.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>These types of developments
led to the wording of the 1<sup>st</sup>-Amendmant. The point was to remove the
opportunity for the government to legislate morality based on any one groupâ€™s
particular brand of biblical understanding. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle"><!--[if !supportLists]-->2.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp; &nbsp;</span>    <b>21st Century
Repubstianity/Christublicanism â€“</b> An entire segment of the church in the USA has
become so embedded with the Republican Party and â€œConservatismâ€ that they can
no longer see any difference between the two. I was among these people for
years, and I am firmly not one of them now. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->a.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>If one takes a step back from
Washington DC Politics long enough, and walks away from the Hyper-Conservative
Echo Chambers and News Silos, one can begin to see a more nuanced view of
American Politics. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->b.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>I used to say that â€œYou
cannot be a Christian and a Democrat, you have to pick oneâ€<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->c.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>I now say, â€œThat, but you
canâ€™t be a Republican eitherâ€. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->d.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>The truth is, both the
Hyper-Liberal and Hyper-Conservative movements are equal and opposite errors
and both enemies of the Cross. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->e.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Both movements advocate for
positions that early Yeshua Followers would have identified with if they time-traveling
to the 21<sup>st</sup> Century. Both movements advocate for positions that
early Yeshua Followers could never have supported. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->f.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Prophetic Voice: As long as
The Church takes sides, we lose our prophetic voice. The longer I step away,
the more clearly I see (as scales coming off my eyes) just how bereft of the
Holy Spirit the â€œConservativeâ€ movement in the USA actually is. We must remain in
the messy middle, speaking truth to power, in love, by prophetic unction, to
both sides.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->g.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Community: Meanwhile, we
must lay down our pitchforks, stop storming Washington DC to get our ideas of
morality forced on non-Yeshua-Followers, and get to the business of quietly serving
our communities. While exceptions exist, the church is invisible on the streets
of America. Where we are visible, the visibility is not good. When we start
being known for our love for one another, for our fellow humans just because
theyâ€™re humans who Yeshua loves (not because they agree with us), we will again
take up our place as the light on the hill. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->h.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>The world doesnâ€™t need The
Church yelling at them telling them to behave like Kingdom People before they
even become Kingdom People. The world needs a hospital wing inviting the
hurting and broken to be loved, cared for, and nursed to health, and then invited
to become Kingdom People. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l1 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->i.<span style="font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Change: Let the Holy Spirit
and Discipleship (which means people already IN the Kingdom) be the places we
talk about how to be Kingdom Citizens. Letâ€™s not try to force Babylon to act
like Jerusalem, when Jerusalem canâ€™t even act like Jerusalem before Yeshua
returns to take up his place as King. <o:p></o:p></p>

<div><br /></div><div><br /></div><div><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><br clear="all" />

<hr align="left" size="1" width="33%" />

<!--[endif]-->

<div id="ftn1">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftnref1" name="_ftn1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;GLXzdaNP&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;The
Editorial Board, \\uc0\\u8220{}Opinion | Does the
\\uc0\\u8216{}Don\\uc0\\u8217{}t Say Gay\\uc0\\u8217{} Bill Say
That?,\\uc0\\u8221{} {\\i{}Wall Street Journal}, March 11, 2022, sec. Opinion,
https://www.wsj.com/articles/does-the-dont-say-gay-bill-say-that-11647041916.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;The
Editorial Board, â€œOpinion | Does the â€˜Donâ€™t Say Gayâ€™ Bill Say That?,â€ Wall
Street Journal, March 11, 2022, sec. Opinion, https://www.wsj.com/articles/does-the-dont-say-gay-bill-say-that-11647041916.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:5},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2096,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VSHNICK8&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2096,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;article-newspaper&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;The
reaction to Floridaâ€™s law on parents and schools is overwrought.&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;Wall
Street
Journal&quot;,&quot;ISSN&quot;:&quot;0099-9660&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;en-US&quot;,&quot;section&quot;:&quot;Opinion&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;www.wsj.com&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Opinion
| Does the â€˜Donâ€™t Say Gayâ€™ Bill Say
That?&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://www.wsj.com/articles/does-the-dont-say-gay-bill-say-that-11647041916&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Board&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;The
Editorial&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2022&quot;,3,30]]},&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2022&quot;,3,11]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->The
Editorial Board, â€œOpinion | Does the â€˜Donâ€™t Say Gayâ€™ Bill Say That?,â€ <i>Wall
Street Journal</i>, March 11, 2022, sec. Opinion,
https://www.wsj.com/articles/does-the-dont-say-gay-bill-say-that-11647041916.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn2">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftnref2" name="_ftn2" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;UueMikit&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Scott
B. Rae, {\\i{}Moral Choices: An Introduction to Ethics}, 3rd ed (Grand Rapids,
Mich: Zondervan, 2009), 19.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Scott B. Rae,
Moral Choices: An Introduction to Ethics, 3rd ed (Grand Rapids, Mich:
Zondervan, 2009),
19.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:6},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:1939,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/RR9K4L92&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:1939,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BJ1012
.R32 2009&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;3rd
ed&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Grand Rapids,
Mich&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-29109-1&quot;,&quot;note&quot;:&quot;OCLC:
ocn286514965&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;375&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Zondervan&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids, Mich&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Moral choices: an introduction to
ethics&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Moral
choices&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Rae&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Scott
B.&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2009&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;19&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Scott
B. Rae, <i>Moral Choices: An Introduction to Ethics</i>, 3rd ed (Grand Rapids,
Mich: Zondervan, 2009), 19.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn3">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftnref3" name="_ftn3" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;O12KHSmG&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;\\uc0\\u8220{}Does
the First Amendment Separate Church and State? | History News
Network,\\uc0\\u8221{} accessed March 30, 2022,
https://historynewsnetwork.org/article/132813.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;â€œDoes
the First Amendment Separate Church and State? | History News Network,â€
accessed March 30, 2022, https://historynewsnetwork.org/article/132813.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:7},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2098,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/TQ7TUSTW&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2098,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;webpage&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Does
the First Amendment Separate Church and State? | History News
Network&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://historynewsnetwork.org/article/132813&quot;,&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2022&quot;,3,30]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->â€œDoes
the First Amendment Separate Church and State? | History News Network,â€
accessed March 30, 2022, https://historynewsnetwork.org/article/132813.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn4">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftnref4" name="_ftn4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;38ZpnHjH&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Charles
Allyn Russell, \\uc0\\u8220{}A History of the Regular Baptists in Rhode Island,
1825-1931\\uc0\\u8221{} (Dissertation, Boston University Graduate School,
1959), https://open.bu.edu/handle/2144/22499.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Charles
Allyn Russell, â€œA History of the Regular Baptists in Rhode Island, 1825-1931â€
(Dissertation, Boston University Graduate School, 1959),
https://open.bu.edu/handle/2144/22499.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:8},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2100,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/QD947HHA&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2100,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;thesis&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;The
story of Baptist beginnings in Rhode Island through Roger Williams and John
Clarke is a familiar one. The writer of this dissertation, while reviewing the
early and familiar chapter of Baptist beginnings in America, concentrates upon
the more recent and neglected period from 1825 to 1931. The result of the
research is the first state history of Rhode Island Baptists. The principal
primary sources for this study have been the annual minutes of the Rhode Island
Baptist State Convention; the yearbooks of the Warren, Providence,
Narragansett, and Roger Williams associations; and the minutes of seven
representative Baptist churches. State, city, and town histories have also been
consulted as well as Rhode Island newspapers and contemporary Baptist magazines
and journals.
[TRUNCATED]&quot;,&quot;genre&quot;:&quot;Dissertation&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;en_US&quot;,&quot;note&quot;:&quot;Accepted:
2017-06-20T19:13:15Z\npublisher: Boston University&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Boston
University Graduate School&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;open.bu.edu&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;A
history of the regular Baptists in Rhode Island,
1825-1931&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://open.bu.edu/handle/2144/22499&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Russell&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Charles
Allyn&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2022&quot;,3,30]]},&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;1959&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Charles
Allyn Russell, â€œA History of the Regular Baptists in Rhode Island, 1825-1931â€
(Dissertation, Boston University Graduate School, 1959),
https://open.bu.edu/handle/2144/22499.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn5">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/My%20Drive%20(darrell.wolfe@student.tku.edu)/-TKU/Courses/Christian%20Ethics/1.%20Christian%20Ethics%20-%20Discussion%20Posts.docx#_ftnref5" name="_ftn5" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-font-family: Arial; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[5]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;KwvbOsdZ&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Darrell
Wolfe, \\uc0\\u8220{}No Hiding.Faith/Resources,\\uc0\\u8221{} No Hiding, accessed
March 30, 2022,
http://www.nohiding.faith/p/resources.html.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Darrell
Wolfe, â€œNo Hiding.Faith/Resources,â€ No Hiding, accessed March 30, 2022,
http://www.nohiding.faith/p/resources.html.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:9},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2103,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/LZ8ZFQF4&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2103,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;webpage&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;Resources<span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>Â Â  </span>An ever-growing list of scholars, articles,
blogs, podcasts, and helpful scholarship around the web.&nbsp; See disclosures
at the bot...&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;No
Hiding&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;No
Hiding.Faith/Resources&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;http://www.nohiding.faith/p/resources.html&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Wolfe&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Darrell&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2022&quot;,3,30]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Darrell
Wolfe, â€œNo Hiding.Faith/Resources,â€ No Hiding, accessed March 30, 2022,
http://www.nohiding.faith/p/resources.html.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

</div>
